title: Add a Container Health Check
description:
## Configure a Runtime Health Check

Give a container a health check so Docker can report whether it is actually working, not just running.

**Requirements:**
1. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`checked`** in the background (keep it alive)
2. Configure a health check on it at run time (a `--health-cmd`)

> 💡 `docker run -d --name checked --health-cmd='true' --health-interval=2s alpine sleep 3600`. A configured check appears under `.Config.Healthcheck`; after the first interval the status under `.State.Health.Status` becomes `healthy`.

hints.title: Attach a health check at run time
hints.body: Use --health-cmd (and optionally --health-interval) to define the probe without rebuilding the image.

hints.title: Read the health status
hints.body: Inspect the live health status once the first check has run.

validation.description: Checks that 'checked' is running and has a health check configured.
